1	# BIM AI — Master Workpackage Tracker
2	
3	> **Prompt files** for each active wave live in `spec/prompts/wave-NN/`. After a wave is fully merged, delete that directory and commit the deletion.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Status Legend
8	
9	| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
10	| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
11	| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
12	| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
13	| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
14	| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |
15	
16	## Done Rule
17	
18	A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) TypeScript typechecks clean (`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`); (b) all new logic has vitest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Wave Map — Overview
23	
24	| Wave                                       | Theme                                              | Status      |
25	| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
26	| Wave 0                                     | Meta / Bootstrap — tracker, docs                   | `done`      |
27	| Wave 1                                     | Interaction Foundation + Plan Canvas               | `done`      |
28	| Wave 2                                     | View System + Wall Depth + Openings                | `done`      |
29	| [Wave 3](#wave-3--element-depth)           | Element Depth (Structural, Curtain Wall, Ceilings) | `done`      |
30	| [Wave 4](#wave-4--data-layer)              | Data Layer (Rooms V2, Parameters, Schedules)       | `done`      |
31	| [Wave 5](#wave-5--families--collaboration) | Families + Collaboration                           | `done`      |
32	| [Wave 6](#wave-6--coordination--export)    | Coordination + Export                              | `done`      |
33	
34	_Waves 0–2 fully merged to main. Prompt files deleted._
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Wave 3 — Element Depth
39	
40	**Goal:** Add structural columns/beams, curtain wall grid config, and ceilings.
41	
42	**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-03/`
43	
44	### Execution order
45	
46	```
47	Batch A (parallel — changes in different file sections):
48	  WP-V2-07    feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall    core/index.ts wall block, meshBuilders inner, i18n inspector.fields
49	  WP-V2-06    feat/wp-v2-06-structural      core/index.ts ElemKind+union, tool chain, meshBuilders end, i18n tools
50	
51	Batch B (after A merged):
52	  WP-V2-08    feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings        depends on column/beam entries added by WP-V2-06
53	```
54	
55	| WP       | Title                                                            | Branch                       | State  | Depends on      |
56	| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------ | --------------- |
57	| WP-V2-07 | Curtain Wall grid params + Inspector (vCount/hCount fields)      | `feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall` | `done` | —               |
58	| WP-V2-06 | Structural Elements (column + beam: types, tools, mesh builders) | `feat/wp-v2-06-structural`   | `done` | —               |
59	| WP-V2-08 | Ceilings (sketch ceiling, plan outline, 3D slab)                 | `feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings`     | `done` | WP-V2-06 merged |
60	
61	---
62	
63	## Wave 4 — Data Layer
64	
65	**Goal:** Rooms V2, Shared Parameters, schedule depth (sorting/grouping/totals, Color Fill Legend).
66	
67	**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-04/`
68	
69	| WP       | Title                                                                                                   | State  |
70	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
71	| WP-V2-09 | Room + Area V2 (Room Bounding, Color Fill, separation lines, deletion via schedule)                     | `done` |
72	| WP-V2-10 | Parameters + Shared Parameters (Shared Params file, Project Params, Calculate Totals, Sorting/Grouping) | `done` |
73	
74	### WP-V2-09 — Room + Area V2
75	
76	**Scope:**
77	
78	- **Room Bounding on linked elements:** Property on linked model instances; when `roomBounding: true`, room detection sees those walls as boundaries.
79	- **Room separation lines:** Virtual 2D line element that acts as a room boundary without physical geometry.
80	- **Room deletion via Schedule:** Deleting a room sticker on the canvas marks it hidden only; true deletion requires Schedule → delete row. UI warning on canvas delete.
81	- **Color Fill Legend:** `color_fill_legend` element placed on a plan view; scheme = field on `room` (e.g. `Zone Program`); floor plan fills rooms automatically. Legend box shows color ↔ value mapping.
82	- **Room upper limit + volume:** Room stores `upperLimitLevelId` + `upperLimitOffsetMm`; `volumeM3` derived from area × height.
83	
84	### WP-V2-10 — Parameters + Shared Parameters
85	
86	**Scope:**
87	
88	- **Shared Parameters:** `SharedParamFile` model with `groups[]` → `parameters[]` (name, dataType, GUID); backend stores/serves as project resource. UI: Manage → Shared Parameters dialog.
89	- **Project Parameters:** Assign a shared param (or internal param) to one or more categories; result appears in Inspector and schedules for those elements.
90	- **Calculate Totals:** Schedule field `aggregation: 'sum' | 'average' | 'min' | 'max' | 'count' | null`; rendered as footer row in schedule table.
91	- **Schedule Sorting/Grouping:** `sortFields[]` with `ascending` flag; `groupFields[]` with `showHeader` and `showBlankLine` flags; backend orders schedule rows accordingly.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## Wave 5 — Families + Collaboration
96	
97	**Goal:** Family Editor MVP, Worksets, Copy/Monitor.
98	
99	**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-05/` — ready. Both WPs run in parallel (Batch A).
100	
101	| WP       | Title                                                                                             | State  |
102	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
103	| WP-V2-11 | Family Editor V2 (template env, parametric dims + EQ, type params, void geometry, profile family) | `done` |
104	| WP-V2-12 | Collaboration V2 (Worksets, Copy/Monitor, Synchronize, Purge Unused, Starting View)               | `done` |
105	
106	### WP-V2-11 — Family Editor V2
107	
108	**Scope:** Dedicated family editor mode (separate route `/family-editor`); family template chooser (Generic Model, Door, Window, Profile); reference planes with parametric dimensions and EQ symmetry constraint; named type parameters with formula support (`Glass Thickness = Width * 0.05`); void extrusion with `cutHost: true` flag; Profile family for use in hosted sweeps; Load into Project button → injects family into project's family library.
109	
110	### WP-V2-12 — Collaboration V2
111	
112	**Scope:**
113	
114	- **Worksets:** Named worksets per project; `worksetId` on each element; Inspector shows Workset field; Properties panel allows batch workset assignment via multi-select.
115	- **Copy/Monitor:** Select a linked model → copy level/grid elements into host model with `monitorSourceId` pointer; change detection marks monitored element with warning badge when source changes.
116	- **Purge Unused:** API endpoint that removes unreferenced types/materials/families; UI confirms list before deletion; runs 3 passes for nested objects.
117	- **Starting View:** Project setting — which view opens on file load.
118	
119	---
120	
121	## Wave 6 — Coordination + Export
122	
123	**Goal:** Selection Sets, Clash Detection, NWC/coordination bundle export.
124	
125	**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-06/` — ready. WP-V2-13 is the only WP.
126	
127	| WP       | Title                                                                                       | State  |
128	| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
129	| WP-V2-13 | Coordination + Clash Detection (Selection Sets, Find Items by Rule, Clash Test, Viewpoints) | `done` |
130	
131	### WP-V2-13 — Coordination + Clash Detection
132	
133	**Scope:**
134	
135	- **Selection Sets:** Named saved selections by filter rule (Category = X, Level = Y, TypeName contains Z); stored per-project; accessible from Project Browser.
136	- **Find Items by Rule:** Select Same Type / Select Same Level / Select Same Category from right-click context menu.
137	- **Clash Detection:** Clash test configuration (Set A vs Set B, tolerance mm); `POST /api/models/:id/clash-tests` runs server-side AABB + mesh proximity test; results list with element pair + distance; clicking a result flies the 3D camera to the clash location.
138	- **Viewpoints with saved visibility state:** Extend existing viewpoint schema to include `hiddenElementIds[]` and `isolatedElementIds[]`; restore on viewpoint activation.
139	
140	---
141	
142	## Update Protocol
143	
144	1. When a WP is completed: change `open` → `done` in the table.
145	2. When starting a wave: create `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` with one file per WP plus a `README.md`.
146	3. When a wave is fully merged: delete `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` and commit the deletion.

# BIM AI — Master Workpackage Tracker

> **Prompt files** for each active wave live in `spec/prompts/wave-NN/`. After a wave is fully merged, delete that directory and commit the deletion.

---

## Status Legend

| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) TypeScript typechecks clean (`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`); (b) all new logic has vitest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

---

## Wave Map — Overview

| Wave                                       | Theme                                              | Status      |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| Wave 0                                     | Meta / Bootstrap — tracker, docs                   | `done`      |
| Wave 1                                     | Interaction Foundation + Plan Canvas               | `done`      |
| Wave 2                                     | View System + Wall Depth + Openings                | `done`      |
| [Wave 3](#wave-3--element-depth)           | Element Depth (Structural, Curtain Wall, Ceilings) | `done`      |
| [Wave 4](#wave-4--data-layer)              | Data Layer (Rooms V2, Parameters, Schedules)       | `done`      |
| [Wave 5](#wave-5--families--collaboration) | Families + Collaboration                           | `done`      |
| [Wave 6](#wave-6--coordination--export)    | Coordination + Export                              | `done`      |

_Waves 0–2 fully merged to main. Prompt files deleted._

---

## Wave 3 — Element Depth

**Goal:** Add structural columns/beams, curtain wall grid config, and ceilings.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-03/`

### Execution order

```
Batch A (parallel — changes in different file sections):
  WP-V2-07    feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall    core/index.ts wall block, meshBuilders inner, i18n inspector.fields
  WP-V2-06    feat/wp-v2-06-structural      core/index.ts ElemKind+union, tool chain, meshBuilders end, i18n tools

Batch B (after A merged):
  WP-V2-08    feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings        depends on column/beam entries added by WP-V2-06
```

| WP       | Title                                                            | Branch                       | State  | Depends on      |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------ | --------------- |
| WP-V2-07 | Curtain Wall grid params + Inspector (vCount/hCount fields)      | `feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall` | `done` | —               |
| WP-V2-06 | Structural Elements (column + beam: types, tools, mesh builders) | `feat/wp-v2-06-structural`   | `done` | —               |
| WP-V2-08 | Ceilings (sketch ceiling, plan outline, 3D slab)                 | `feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings`     | `done` | WP-V2-06 merged |

---

## Wave 4 — Data Layer

**Goal:** Rooms V2, Shared Parameters, schedule depth (sorting/grouping/totals, Color Fill Legend).

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-04/`

| WP       | Title                                                                                                   | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-09 | Room + Area V2 (Room Bounding, Color Fill, separation lines, deletion via schedule)                     | `done` |
| WP-V2-10 | Parameters + Shared Parameters (Shared Params file, Project Params, Calculate Totals, Sorting/Grouping) | `done` |

### WP-V2-09 — Room + Area V2

**Scope:**

- **Room Bounding on linked elements:** Property on linked model instances; when `roomBounding: true`, room detection sees those walls as boundaries.
- **Room separation lines:** Virtual 2D line element that acts as a room boundary without physical geometry.
- **Room deletion via Schedule:** Deleting a room sticker on the canvas marks it hidden only; true deletion requires Schedule → delete row. UI warning on canvas delete.
- **Color Fill Legend:** `color_fill_legend` element placed on a plan view; scheme = field on `room` (e.g. `Zone Program`); floor plan fills rooms automatically. Legend box shows color ↔ value mapping.
- **Room upper limit + volume:** Room stores `upperLimitLevelId` + `upperLimitOffsetMm`; `volumeM3` derived from area × height.

### WP-V2-10 — Parameters + Shared Parameters

**Scope:**

- **Shared Parameters:** `SharedParamFile` model with `groups[]` → `parameters[]` (name, dataType, GUID); backend stores/serves as project resource. UI: Manage → Shared Parameters dialog.
- **Project Parameters:** Assign a shared param (or internal param) to one or more categories; result appears in Inspector and schedules for those elements.
- **Calculate Totals:** Schedule field `aggregation: 'sum' | 'average' | 'min' | 'max' | 'count' | null`; rendered as footer row in schedule table.
- **Schedule Sorting/Grouping:** `sortFields[]` with `ascending` flag; `groupFields[]` with `showHeader` and `showBlankLine` flags; backend orders schedule rows accordingly.

---

## Wave 5 — Families + Collaboration

**Goal:** Family Editor MVP, Worksets, Copy/Monitor.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-05/` — ready. Both WPs run in parallel (Batch A).

| WP       | Title                                                                                             | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-11 | Family Editor V2 (template env, parametric dims + EQ, type params, void geometry, profile family) | `done` |
| WP-V2-12 | Collaboration V2 (Worksets, Copy/Monitor, Synchronize, Purge Unused, Starting View)               | `done` |

### WP-V2-11 — Family Editor V2

**Scope:** Dedicated family editor mode (separate route `/family-editor`); family template chooser (Generic Model, Door, Window, Profile); reference planes with parametric dimensions and EQ symmetry constraint; named type parameters with formula support (`Glass Thickness = Width * 0.05`); void extrusion with `cutHost: true` flag; Profile family for use in hosted sweeps; Load into Project button → injects family into project's family library.

### WP-V2-12 — Collaboration V2

**Scope:**

- **Worksets:** Named worksets per project; `worksetId` on each element; Inspector shows Workset field; Properties panel allows batch workset assignment via multi-select.
- **Copy/Monitor:** Select a linked model → copy level/grid elements into host model with `monitorSourceId` pointer; change detection marks monitored element with warning badge when source changes.
- **Purge Unused:** API endpoint that removes unreferenced types/materials/families; UI confirms list before deletion; runs 3 passes for nested objects.
- **Starting View:** Project setting — which view opens on file load.

---

## Wave 6 — Coordination + Export

**Goal:** Selection Sets, Clash Detection, NWC/coordination bundle export.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-06/` — ready. WP-V2-13 is the only WP.

| WP       | Title                                                                                       | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-13 | Coordination + Clash Detection (Selection Sets, Find Items by Rule, Clash Test, Viewpoints) | `done` |

### WP-V2-13 — Coordination + Clash Detection

**Scope:**

- **Selection Sets:** Named saved selections by filter rule (Category = X, Level = Y, TypeName contains Z); stored per-project; accessible from Project Browser.
- **Find Items by Rule:** Select Same Type / Select Same Level / Select Same Category from right-click context menu.
- **Clash Detection:** Clash test configuration (Set A vs Set B, tolerance mm); `POST /api/models/:id/clash-tests` runs server-side AABB + mesh proximity test; results list with element pair + distance; clicking a result flies the 3D camera to the clash location.
- **Viewpoints with saved visibility state:** Extend existing viewpoint schema to include `hiddenElementIds[]` and `isolatedElementIds[]`; restore on viewpoint activation.

---

## Update Protocol

1. When a WP is completed: change `open` → `done` in the table.
2. When starting a wave: create `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` with one file per WP plus a `README.md`.
3. When a wave is fully merged: delete `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` and commit the deletion.

> **Prompt files** for each active wave live in `spec/prompts/wave-NN/`. After a wave is fully merged, delete that directory and commit the deletion.

---

## Status Legend

| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) TypeScript typechecks clean (`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`); (b) all new logic has vitest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

---

## Wave Map — Overview

| Wave                                       | Theme                                              | Status      |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| Wave 0                                     | Meta / Bootstrap — tracker, docs                   | `done`      |
| Wave 1                                     | Interaction Foundation + Plan Canvas               | `done`      |
| Wave 2                                     | View System + Wall Depth + Openings                | `done`      |
| [Wave 3](#wave-3--element-depth)           | Element Depth (Structural, Curtain Wall, Ceilings) | `done`      |

| [Wave 5](#wave-5--families--collaboration) | Families + Collaboration                           | `done`      |

_Waves 0–2 fully merged to main. Prompt files deleted._

---

## Wave 3 — Element Depth

**Goal:** Add structural columns/beams, curtain wall grid config, and ceilings.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-03/`

### Execution order

```
Batch A (parallel — changes in different file sections):
  WP-V2-07    feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall    core/index.ts wall block, meshBuilders inner, i18n inspector.fields
  WP-V2-06    feat/wp-v2-06-structural      core/index.ts ElemKind+union, tool chain, meshBuilders end, i18n tools

Batch B (after A merged):
  WP-V2-08    feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings        depends on column/beam entries added by WP-V2-06
```

| WP       | Title                                                            | Branch                       | State  | Depends on      |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------ | --------------- |
| WP-V2-07 | Curtain Wall grid params + Inspector (vCount/hCount fields)      | `feat/wp-v2-07-curtain-wall` | `done` | —               |
| WP-V2-06 | Structural Elements (column + beam: types, tools, mesh builders) | `feat/wp-v2-06-structural`   | `done` | —               |
| WP-V2-08 | Ceilings (sketch ceiling, plan outline, 3D slab)                 | `feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings`     | `done` | WP-V2-06 merged |

---

## Wave 4 — Data Layer

**Goal:** Rooms V2, Shared Parameters, schedule depth (sorting/grouping/totals, Color Fill Legend).

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-04/`

| WP       | Title                                                                                                   | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-09 | Room + Area V2 (Room Bounding, Color Fill, separation lines, deletion via schedule)                     | `done` |
| WP-V2-10 | Parameters + Shared Parameters (Shared Params file, Project Params, Calculate Totals, Sorting/Grouping) | `done` |

### WP-V2-09 — Room + Area V2

**Scope:**

- **Room Bounding on linked elements:** Property on linked model instances; when `roomBounding: true`, room detection sees those walls as boundaries.
- **Room separation lines:** Virtual 2D line element that acts as a room boundary without physical geometry.
- **Room deletion via Schedule:** Deleting a room sticker on the canvas marks it hidden only; true deletion requires Schedule → delete row. UI warning on canvas delete.
- **Color Fill Legend:** `color_fill_legend` element placed on a plan view; scheme = field on `room` (e.g. `Zone Program`); floor plan fills rooms automatically. Legend box shows color ↔ value mapping.
- **Room upper limit + volume:** Room stores `upperLimitLevelId` + `upperLimitOffsetMm`; `volumeM3` derived from area × height.

### WP-V2-10 — Parameters + Shared Parameters

**Scope:**

- **Shared Parameters:** `SharedParamFile` model with `groups[]` → `parameters[]` (name, dataType, GUID); backend stores/serves as project resource. UI: Manage → Shared Parameters dialog.
- **Project Parameters:** Assign a shared param (or internal param) to one or more categories; result appears in Inspector and schedules for those elements.
- **Calculate Totals:** Schedule field `aggregation: 'sum' | 'average' | 'min' | 'max' | 'count' | null`; rendered as footer row in schedule table.
- **Schedule Sorting/Grouping:** `sortFields[]` with `ascending` flag; `groupFields[]` with `showHeader` and `showBlankLine` flags; backend orders schedule rows accordingly.

---

## Wave 5 — Families + Collaboration

**Goal:** Family Editor MVP, Worksets, Copy/Monitor.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-05/` — ready. Both WPs run in parallel (Batch A).

| WP       | Title                                                                                             | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-11 | Family Editor V2 (template env, parametric dims + EQ, type params, void geometry, profile family) | `done` |
| WP-V2-12 | Collaboration V2 (Worksets, Copy/Monitor, Synchronize, Purge Unused, Starting View)               | `done` |

### WP-V2-11 — Family Editor V2

**Scope:** Dedicated family editor mode (separate route `/family-editor`); family template chooser (Generic Model, Door, Window, Profile); reference planes with parametric dimensions and EQ symmetry constraint; named type parameters with formula support (`Glass Thickness = Width * 0.05`); void extrusion with `cutHost: true` flag; Profile family for use in hosted sweeps; Load into Project button → injects family into project's family library.

### WP-V2-12 — Collaboration V2

**Scope:**

- **Worksets:** Named worksets per project; `worksetId` on each element; Inspector shows Workset field; Properties panel allows batch workset assignment via multi-select.
- **Copy/Monitor:** Select a linked model → copy level/grid elements into host model with `monitorSourceId` pointer; change detection marks monitored element with warning badge when source changes.
- **Purge Unused:** API endpoint that removes unreferenced types/materials/families; UI confirms list before deletion; runs 3 passes for nested objects.
- **Starting View:** Project setting — which view opens on file load.

---

## Wave 6 — Coordination + Export

**Goal:** Selection Sets, Clash Detection, NWC/coordination bundle export.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-06/` — ready. WP-V2-13 is the only WP.

| WP       | Title                                                                                       | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-13 | Coordination + Clash Detection (Selection Sets, Find Items by Rule, Clash Test, Viewpoints) | `done` |

### WP-V2-13 — Coordination + Clash Detection

**Scope:**

- **Selection Sets:** Named saved selections by filter rule (Category = X, Level = Y, TypeName contains Z); stored per-project; accessible from Project Browser.
- **Find Items by Rule:** Select Same Type / Select Same Level / Select Same Category from right-click context menu.
- **Clash Detection:** Clash test configuration (Set A vs Set B, tolerance mm); `POST /api/models/:id/clash-tests` runs server-side AABB + mesh proximity test; results list with element pair + distance; clicking a result flies the 3D camera to the clash location.
- **Viewpoints with saved visibility state:** Extend existing viewpoint schema to include `hiddenElementIds[]` and `isolatedElementIds[]`; restore on viewpoint activation.

---

## Update Protocol

1. When a WP is completed: change `open` → `done` in the table.
2. When starting a wave: create `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` with one file per WP plus a `README.md`.
3. When a wave is fully merged: delete `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` and commit the deletion.

| WP-V2-08 | Ceilings (sketch ceiling, plan outline, 3D slab)                 | `feat/wp-v2-08-ceilings`     | `done` | WP-V2-06 merged |

---

## Wave 4 — Data Layer

**Goal:** Rooms V2, Shared Parameters, schedule depth (sorting/grouping/totals, Color Fill Legend).

| WP       | Title                                                                                                   | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-09 | Room + Area V2 (Room Bounding, Color Fill, separation lines, deletion via schedule)                     | `done` |
| WP-V2-10 | Parameters + Shared Parameters (Shared Params file, Project Params, Calculate Totals, Sorting/Grouping) | `done` |

### WP-V2-09 — Room + Area V2

**Scope:**

- **Room Bounding on linked elements:** Property on linked model instances; when `roomBounding: true`, room detection sees those walls as boundaries.
- **Room separation lines:** Virtual 2D line element that acts as a room boundary without physical geometry.
- **Room deletion via Schedule:** Deleting a room sticker on the canvas marks it hidden only; true deletion requires Schedule → delete row. UI warning on canvas delete.
- **Color Fill Legend:** `color_fill_legend` element placed on a plan view; scheme = field on `room` (e.g. `Zone Program`); floor plan fills rooms automatically. Legend box shows color ↔ value mapping.
- **Room upper limit + volume:** Room stores `upperLimitLevelId` + `upperLimitOffsetMm`; `volumeM3` derived from area × height.

### WP-V2-10 — Parameters + Shared Parameters

**Scope:**

- **Shared Parameters:** `SharedParamFile` model with `groups[]` → `parameters[]` (name, dataType, GUID); backend stores/serves as project resource. UI: Manage → Shared Parameters dialog.
- **Project Parameters:** Assign a shared param (or internal param) to one or more categories; result appears in Inspector and schedules for those elements.
- **Calculate Totals:** Schedule field `aggregation: 'sum' | 'average' | 'min' | 'max' | 'count' | null`; rendered as footer row in schedule table.
- **Schedule Sorting/Grouping:** `sortFields[]` with `ascending` flag; `groupFields[]` with `showHeader` and `showBlankLine` flags; backend orders schedule rows accordingly.

---

## Wave 5 — Families + Collaboration

**Goal:** Family Editor MVP, Worksets, Copy/Monitor.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-05/` — ready. Both WPs run in parallel (Batch A).

| WP       | Title                                                                                             | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-11 | Family Editor V2 (template env, parametric dims + EQ, type params, void geometry, profile family) | `done` |
| WP-V2-12 | Collaboration V2 (Worksets, Copy/Monitor, Synchronize, Purge Unused, Starting View)               | `done` |

### WP-V2-11 — Family Editor V2

**Scope:** Dedicated family editor mode (separate route `/family-editor`); family template chooser (Generic Model, Door, Window, Profile); reference planes with parametric dimensions and EQ symmetry constraint; named type parameters with formula support (`Glass Thickness = Width * 0.05`); void extrusion with `cutHost: true` flag; Profile family for use in hosted sweeps; Load into Project button → injects family into project's family library.

### WP-V2-12 — Collaboration V2

**Scope:**

- **Worksets:** Named worksets per project; `worksetId` on each element; Inspector shows Workset field; Properties panel allows batch workset assignment via multi-select.
- **Copy/Monitor:** Select a linked model → copy level/grid elements into host model with `monitorSourceId` pointer; change detection marks monitored element with warning badge when source changes.
- **Purge Unused:** API endpoint that removes unreferenced types/materials/families; UI confirms list before deletion; runs 3 passes for nested objects.
- **Starting View:** Project setting — which view opens on file load.

---

## Wave 6 — Coordination + Export

**Goal:** Selection Sets, Clash Detection, NWC/coordination bundle export.

**Prompt files:** `spec/prompts/wave-06/` — ready. WP-V2-13 is the only WP.

| WP       | Title                                                                                       | State  |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ |
| WP-V2-13 | Coordination + Clash Detection (Selection Sets, Find Items by Rule, Clash Test, Viewpoints) | `done` |

### WP-V2-13 — Coordination + Clash Detection

**Scope:**

- **Selection Sets:** Named saved selections by filter rule (Category = X, Level = Y, TypeName contains Z); stored per-project; accessible from Project Browser.
- **Find Items by Rule:** Select Same Type / Select Same Level / Select Same Category from right-click context menu.
- **Clash Detection:** Clash test configuration (Set A vs Set B, tolerance mm); `POST /api/models/:id/clash-tests` runs server-side AABB + mesh proximity test; results list with element pair + distance; clicking a result flies the 3D camera to the clash location.
- **Viewpoints with saved visibility state:** Extend existing viewpoint schema to include `hiddenElementIds[]` and `isolatedElementIds[]`; restore on viewpoint activation.

---

## Update Protocol

1. When a WP is completed: change `open` → `done` in the table.
2. When starting a wave: create `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` with one file per WP plus a `README.md`.
3. When a wave is fully merged: delete `spec/prompts/wave-NN/` and commit the deletion.

# BIM AI — Master Workpackage Tracker

> **Prompt files** for each active wave live in `spec/prompts/wave-NN/`. After a wave is fully merged, delete that directory and commit the deletion.

---

## Status Legend

| Symbol     | Meaning                                                         |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `done`     | Meets the done rule — tested, type-clean, merged to main        |
| `partial`  | Some slice exists; measurable progress; spec requirements unmet |
| `open`     | Not started                                                     |
| `deferred` | Explicitly out of scope for current roadmap                     |

## Done Rule

A workpackage is `done` when all of: (a) TypeScript typechecks clean (`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`); (b) all new logic has vitest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed.

---

## Wave Map — Overview

| Wave                                       | Theme                                              | Status      |
| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| Wave 0                                     | Meta / Bootstrap — tracker, docs                   | `done`      |
| Wave 1                                     | Interaction Foundation + Plan Canvas               | `done`      |
| Wave 2                                     | View System + Wall Depth + Openings                | `done`      |
| [Wave 3](#wave-3--element-depth)           | Element Depth (Structural, Curtain Wall, Ceilings) | `done`      |
| [Wave 4](#wave-4--data-layer)              | Data Layer (Rooms V2, Parameters, Schedules)       | `done`      |
| [Wave 5](#wave-5--families--collaboration) | Families + Collaboration                           | `done`      |
| [Wave 6](#wave-6--coordination--export)    | Coordination + Export                              | `done`      |

_All waves merged to main. Prompt files deleted._

---

**Outcomes:** `ok` on success; `merge_id_collision` / `merge_reference_unresolved` when preflight fails (returns the validated **`commands`** list); `sketch_unavailable` when `available` is not true; `invalid_sketch` for wrong `replayKind` or `schemaVersion`; `invalid_command` for a non-list `commands` or any entry whose `type` is not one of `createLevel` / `createFloor` / `createWall` / `createRoof` / `createStair` / `createRoomOutline` / `insertDoorOnWall` / `insertWindowOnWall` / `createSlabOpening`; `constraint_error` when **`try_commit_bundle`** evaluation hits blocking violations. **`exchange_ifc_ids_identity_pset_gap`** / **`exchange_ifc_ids_qto_gap`** may append a deterministic pointer to **`commandSketch.authoritativeReplay_v0.idsAuthoritativeReplayMap_v0`** (space + roof row counts) when the roundtrip gate runs — not a `quickFixCommand` bundle.

**Still not in v0:** arbitrary IFC importers and unconstrained merge outside this authoritative bundle + preflight. **`summarize_kernel_ifc_semantic_roundtrip`** nests replay under **`commandSketch.authoritativeReplay_v0`**.

## Still deferred

- **IfcRoof** beyond prism replay: **`roofTypeId`** not round-tripped; **`gable_pitched_rectangle`** not distinguished in IFC body; roof-hosted voids remain **`slabRoofHostedVoidReplaySkipped_v0`** only.
- Broader QTO roll-ups (full composite takeoffs), materials, classifications, and layered composites.
- **Draco**/mesh-compression on glTF sibling export (tracked under WP‑X02).

When these land, bump `ifcEncoding` commentary / `kernelNote` in [`export_ifc.py`](../app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py) and extend this table.

## Previous explicit deferrals (history)

- ~~Property sets, quantities, materials, classifications.~~ → **Psets (Reference) shipped; narrow QTO + materials remain.**

## Artifact contract

| Resource                     | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET …/exports/ifc-manifest` | **`ifc_manifest_v0`** JSON: parity fields + `ifcEncoding` + `artifactHasGeometryEntities` + `exportedIfcKindsInArtifact` + `ifcSemanticImportScope_v0` + `kernelExpectedIfcKinds` hints |
| `GET …/exports/model.ifc`    | Downloadable IFC STEP — **`bim_ai_ifc_kernel_v1`** kernel solids when **`ifcopenshell`** + eligible geometry exists; **`bim_ai_ifc_empty_shell_v0`** empty DATA otherwise               |

content.text:
I dug into the actual code rather than trusting the doc's self-marked status. Big finding: **`rendering-v2-spec.md` is wildly out of date — almost everything in it is already shipped, the spec just never got updated.**

## `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md` — fully implemented (~95%)

The doc opens with "Status baseline: shadows, AO, edge lines fully absent" and says only R1 is done. That baseline is stale. Verified in code:

| ID | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| R1-01..05 | ✅ done | `Viewport.tsx:194` shadowMap, `:263` EffectComposer, `:267` SSAOPass, `sceneHelpers.ts:80` EdgesGeometry |
| R2-01 | ✅ done | `csgWorker.ts` + `CSG_ENABLED` flag |
| R2-02..03 | ✅ done | `families/geometryFns/{doorGeometry,windowGeometry}.ts` |
| R2-04..05 | ✅ done | `meshBuilders.ts:901,925` (treads/stringers), `:1411,1452` (posts/balusters) |
| R2-06 | ✅ done | `makeRoofMassMesh` |
| R2-07 | ✅ done | `makeFloorSlabMesh` |
| R2-08 | ✅ done | `makeSiteMesh` |
| R3-01..03 | ✅ done | `materials.ts`, env map via `scene.environment`, `addCladdingBoards` |
| R3-04 | ✅ done | `OutlinePass` in Viewport |
| R4-01 | ✅ done | `SectionBox` + `clippingPlanes` |
| R4-02 | ✅ done | `OrthographicCamera` + `orthoMode` state |
| FL-01..05, FL-07 | ✅ done | `familyTypeId` field, `familyCatalog.ts`, `FamilyEditorWorkbench`, Inspector type selector |
| FL-06 | ❓ no dedicated `FamilyLibraryPanel.tsx` component |
| FL-08 | 🟡 partial | `MaterialLayerStackWorkbench` exists but not full system-family stack |

## `spec/target-house-seed.md` — fully implemented (~85%)

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| S1 (`vp-ssw` viewpoint at -5000/-14000/11000) | ✅ `app/scripts/seed.py:494` |
| S2/S3 (white_cladding / white_render in seed) | ✅ |
| S4 (annex 500 mm protrusion) | ✅ `seed.py:51,121` |
| GAP-R1/R2 (white_cladding/render materialKey) | ✅ `meshBuilders.ts:1129,1144` |
| GAP-R3 (gable triangle glazing) | ✅ `meshBuilders.ts:1234` |
| GAP-R4 (balcony + glass balustrade) | ✅ `core/index.ts:397` `kind: 'balcony'` + `makeBalconyMesh` |
| GAP-R6 (flat roof + roof materialKey) | ✅ `meshBuilders.ts:809,837` |
| GAP-R5 (slab edge strip) | ❌ **still open** |
| GAP-R7 (interior visible through glass) | 🟡 spec marks as "aspirational" — no plan |

## Still genuinely partial (explicit open items in the doc)

- **`spec/ifc-export-wp-x03-slice.md`** — "Still deferred": `roofTypeId` roundtrip, gable distinction in IFC body, roof-hosted voids, broader QTO/materials/classifications, Draco mesh compression.
- **`spec/prd/cli-agent-home-design-gaps.md`** — Slice D, P1 (multi-storey/garages/site grid), P2 (`bim-ai export ifc/gltf/json`, `bim-ai diff`), P3 (onboarding).
- **`spec/prd/revit-production-parity-ai-agent-prd.md`** — Phase A–F roadmap, ongoing.

## My recommendation

**Two specs worth retiring or rewriting:**

1. **`rendering-v2-spec.md`** — the work is done; the doc is misleading. Either delete it or replace with a short "Rendering V2 status: shipped — baselines in `e2e/rendering-v2-baselines.spec.ts`" note. FL-06 and FL-08 could move to a one-line backlog entry in the master tracker if you still want them.

2. **`target-house-seed.md`** — gap list is mostly false now. Either delete the "Renderer / Feature Gaps" section (keep the architectural description as a reference seed spec) or shrink the GAP table to just R5 + R7.

The IFC slice and the two PRDs are still useful as living docs since they have legitimate open items.

Want me to slim down `rendering-v2-spec.md` and `target-house-seed.md`, or just delete them outright?

content.content:
1456	### FL-06 · Family library browser panel
1457	
1458	A dedicated panel (accessible from the Project Browser left rail or via ⌘K
1459	`> Browse families`) showing the full built-in catalog plus any user-defined
1460	types:
1461	
1462	```
1463	┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
1464	│  Family Library              [+ New]   │
1465	│  ────────────────────────────────────  │
1466	│  🔍 Search families…                   │
1467	│                                        │
1468	│  ▾ Doors                               │
1469	│    Single Flush Door          4 types  │
1470	│    Double Leaf Door           3 types  │
1471	│    Sliding Door               2 types  │
1472	│  ▾ Windows                             │
1473	│    Fixed Light                3 types  │
1474	│    Casement                   4 types  │
1475	│    Double-Hung                2 types  │
1476	│  ▾ Stairs                              │
1477	│    Straight Stair             2 types  │
1478	│  ▾ Railings                            │
1479	│    Baluster Railing           2 types  │
1480	└────────────────────────────────────────┘
1481	```
1482	
1483	Clicking a type shows a thumbnail preview (rendered via an offscreen
1484	`THREE.WebGLRenderer` into a 128×128 canvas), the parameter list, and a
1485	**"Place"** button that sets the active tool to the corresponding element
1486	draw tool pre-loaded with that type.
1487	
1488	**Thumbnail generation:** On first access per type, render the family geometry
1489	function with default params into an offscreen canvas at 128×128, store as a
1490	blob URL in a module-level cache.
1491	
1492	**Acceptance:** Panel lists all built-in families grouped by discipline.
1493	Search filters by name. Clicking a type shows its params. Thumbnails render
1494	within 200ms (cached after first render). Placing a type from the panel starts
1495	the draw tool with the correct `familyTypeId`.
1496	
1497	---
1498	
1499	### FL-07 · Custom type authoring
1500	
1501	Allow users to create their own types within a built-in family (e.g. a custom
1502	"900 × 2400 — Black Aluminium" door type):
1503	
1504	```
1505	Family:  Single Flush Door
1506	Name:    900 × 2400 Black Alum
1507	────────────────────────────
1508	Width        900 mm
1509	Height      2400 mm
1510	Frame profile  60 mm
1511	Material     Aluminium
1512	Swing        Left
1513	────────────────────────────
1514	[Cancel]              [Save type]
1515	```
1516	
1517	On save, a `family_type` element is added to the project model (not the
1518	catalog) with `isBuiltIn: false`. It appears in the type selector and library
1519	browser alongside built-in types, visually distinguished (e.g. "Custom" badge).
1520	
1521	Custom types are persisted in the project snapshot (`elementsById`). They are
1522	not shared across projects unless exported.
1523	
1524	**Export/import:** A "Share type" action serialises the `family_type` element
1525	to a JSON file that can be imported into another project.
1526	
1527	**Acceptance:** User can create, name, edit, and delete custom types. Custom
1528	types appear in the Inspector type picker and library browser. They persist
1529	across page reloads (in snapshot). JSON export round-trips cleanly.
1530	
1531	---
1532	
1533	### FL-08 · System family layer stack (wall / floor / roof assemblies)
1534	
1535	This is the deepest work and maps to Revit's "System Families." Instead of
1536	walls having a single `thicknessMm` and flat color, a wall type is defined as
1537	an ordered stack of material layers:
1538	
1539	```ts
1540	interface WallLayerAssembly {
1541	  familyId: 'wall_type';
1542	  name: string; // "Ext. Timber Cladding on Timber Frame"
1543	  layers: {
1544	    name: string; // "Cladding", "Air gap", "Frame + Insulation"
1545	    thicknessMm: number;
1546	    materialKey: string; // maps to PBR params + color token
1547	    function: 'structure' | 'insulation' | 'finish' | 'membrane' | 'air';
1548	    exterior?: boolean;
1549	  }[];
1550	}
1551	```
1552	
1553	**Renderer implications:** When a wall has a `familyTypeId` pointing to a
1554	`wall_type`, the renderer extrudes each layer as a separate `BoxGeometry`
1555	offset from the wall centreline. The exterior finish layer renders with cladding
1556	geometry (FL-03 / R3-03). The structural layer renders with its own PBR params.
1557	
1558	**Built-in wall assemblies (minimum viable set):**
1559	
1560	| ID                   | Name                  | Layers                                                                         |
1561	| -------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
1562	| `wall.ext-timber`    | Ext. Timber Frame     | Cladding 18mm + Air gap 25mm + Frame+Ins 140mm + VCL 3mm + Plasterboard 12.5mm |
1563	| `wall.ext-masonry`   | Ext. Brick Cavity     | Brick 102mm + Cavity 75mm + Block 100mm + Plaster 13mm                         |
1564	| `wall.int-partition` | Int. Timber Partition | Plasterboard 12.5mm + Stud 89mm + Plasterboard 12.5mm                          |
1565	| `wall.int-blockwork` | Int. Blockwork        | Plaster 13mm + Block 100mm + Plaster 13mm                                      |
1566	
1567	**Plan view impact:** Layer boundaries should draw as thin lines in the plan
1568	canvas, matching the Revit plan drawing convention where you see the full layer
1569	stack cross-section. This connects to B01 (plan drafting visuals).
1570	
1571	**Acceptance:** A wall with a `wall_type` familyTypeId renders as a multi-layer
1572	extrusion in 3D. Total thickness matches the sum of layer thicknesses. Each
1573	layer has correct color and roughness from its `materialKey`. Plan view shows
1574	layer boundary lines.
1575	
1576	## Performance architecture (implemented 2026-05-06)
1577	
1578	### Problem
1579	
1580	The original Viewport had a single monolithic `useEffect` that **nuked the
1581	entire Three.js scene and rebuilt every mesh synchronously on every state
1582	change** — including every element add, every selection click, and every
1583	category toggle. This was O(N) geometry work per edit, blocking the main
1584	thread until complete.
1585	
1586	Additionally, `makeWallWithOpenings` (CSG boolean subtraction) ran synchronously
1587	on the main thread for every wall that hosted doors or windows, blocking the UI
1588	for hundreds of milliseconds on complex models.
1589	
1590	### Solution
1591	
1592	**Incremental scene manager** (`Viewport.tsx` — geometry `useEffect`):
1593	
1594	- `bimPickMapRef` (`Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>`) serves as the persistent
1595	  mesh cache. Meshes survive across renders; only changed elements are touched.
1596	- On each `elementsById` change the effect **diffs** against `prevElementsByIdRef`
1597	  to compute `addedIds`, `removedIds`, and `changedIds`.
1598	- **Dependency propagation** ensures cascading invalidation:
1599	  - Wall changed → hosted doors + windows also dirty (positions depend on wall)
1600	  - Door/window added/changed/removed → host wall dirty (CSG opening changes)
1601	  - Level elevation changed → all elements at that level dirty
1602	  - Stair changed → hosted railings dirty
1603	- Old meshes in `toRemove` are disposed (geometry + materials) to avoid GPU
1604	  leaks. New meshes for `toRebuild` are built and inserted.
1605	- Result: **O(delta) per edit** instead of O(N).

file.content:
### FL-06 · Family library browser panel

A dedicated panel (accessible from the Project Browser left rail or via ⌘K
`> Browse families`) showing the full built-in catalog plus any user-defined
types:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Family Library              [+ New]   │
│  ────────────────────────────────────  │
│  🔍 Search families…                   │
│                                        │
│  ▾ Doors                               │
│    Single Flush Door          4 types  │
│    Double Leaf Door           3 types  │
│    Sliding Door               2 types  │
│  ▾ Windows                             │
│    Fixed Light                3 types  │
│    Casement                   4 types  │
│    Double-Hung                2 types  │
│  ▾ Stairs                              │
│    Straight Stair             2 types  │
│  ▾ Railings                            │
│    Baluster Railing           2 types  │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Clicking a type shows a thumbnail preview (rendered via an offscreen
`THREE.WebGLRenderer` into a 128×128 canvas), the parameter list, and a
**"Place"** button that sets the active tool to the corresponding element
draw tool pre-loaded with that type.

**Thumbnail generation:** On first access per type, render the family geometry
function with default params into an offscreen canvas at 128×128, store as a
blob URL in a module-level cache.

**Acceptance:** Panel lists all built-in families grouped by discipline.
Search filters by name. Clicking a type shows its params. Thumbnails render
within 200ms (cached after first render). Placing a type from the panel starts
the draw tool with the correct `familyTypeId`.

---

### FL-07 · Custom type authoring

Allow users to create their own types within a built-in family (e.g. a custom
"900 × 2400 — Black Aluminium" door type):

```
Family:  Single Flush Door
Name:    900 × 2400 Black Alum
────────────────────────────
Width        900 mm
Height      2400 mm
Frame profile  60 mm
Material     Aluminium
Swing        Left
────────────────────────────
[Cancel]              [Save type]
```

On save, a `family_type` element is added to the project model (not the
catalog) with `isBuiltIn: false`. It appears in the type selector and library
browser alongside built-in types, visually distinguished (e.g. "Custom" badge).

Custom types are persisted in the project snapshot (`elementsById`). They are
not shared across projects unless exported.

**Export/import:** A "Share type" action serialises the `family_type` element
to a JSON file that can be imported into another project.

**Acceptance:** User can create, name, edit, and delete custom types. Custom
types appear in the Inspector type picker and library browser. They persist
across page reloads (in snapshot). JSON export round-trips cleanly.

---

### FL-08 · System family layer stack (wall / floor / roof assemblies)

This is the deepest work and maps to Revit's "System Families." Instead of
walls having a single `thicknessMm` and flat color, a wall type is defined as
an ordered stack of material layers:

```ts
interface WallLayerAssembly {
  familyId: 'wall_type';
  name: string; // "Ext. Timber Cladding on Timber Frame"
  layers: {
    name: string; // "Cladding", "Air gap", "Frame + Insulation"
    thicknessMm: number;
    materialKey: string; // maps to PBR params + color token
    function: 'structure' | 'insulation' | 'finish' | 'membrane' | 'air';
    exterior?: boolean;
  }[];
}
```

**Renderer implications:** When a wall has a `familyTypeId` pointing to a
`wall_type`, the renderer extrudes each layer as a separate `BoxGeometry`
offset from the wall centreline. The exterior finish layer renders with cladding
geometry (FL-03 / R3-03). The structural layer renders with its own PBR params.

**Built-in wall assemblies (minimum viable set):**

| ID                   | Name                  | Layers                                                                         |
| -------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `wall.ext-timber`    | Ext. Timber Frame     | Cladding 18mm + Air gap 25mm + Frame+Ins 140mm + VCL 3mm + Plasterboard 12.5mm |
| `wall.ext-masonry`   | Ext. Brick Cavity     | Brick 102mm + Cavity 75mm + Block 100mm + Plaster 13mm                         |
| `wall.int-partition` | Int. Timber Partition | Plasterboard 12.5mm + Stud 89mm + Plasterboard 12.5mm                          |
| `wall.int-blockwork` | Int. Blockwork        | Plaster 13mm + Block 100mm + Plaster 13mm                                      |

**Plan view impact:** Layer boundaries should draw as thin lines in the plan
canvas, matching the Revit plan drawing convention where you see the full layer
stack cross-section. This connects to B01 (plan drafting visuals).

**Acceptance:** A wall with a `wall_type` familyTypeId renders as a multi-layer
extrusion in 3D. Total thickness matches the sum of layer thicknesses. Each
layer has correct color and roughness from its `materialKey`. Plan view shows
layer boundary lines.

## Performance architecture (implemented 2026-05-06)

### Problem

The original Viewport had a single monolithic `useEffect` that **nuked the
entire Three.js scene and rebuilt every mesh synchronously on every state
change** — including every element add, every selection click, and every
category toggle. This was O(N) geometry work per edit, blocking the main
thread until complete.

Additionally, `makeWallWithOpenings` (CSG boolean subtraction) ran synchronously
on the main thread for every wall that hosted doors or windows, blocking the UI
for hundreds of milliseconds on complex models.

### Solution

**Incremental scene manager** (`Viewport.tsx` — geometry `useEffect`):

- `bimPickMapRef` (`Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>`) serves as the persistent
  mesh cache. Meshes survive across renders; only changed elements are touched.
- On each `elementsById` change the effect **diffs** against `prevElementsByIdRef`
  to compute `addedIds`, `removedIds`, and `changedIds`.
- **Dependency propagation** ensures cascading invalidation:
  - Wall changed → hosted doors + windows also dirty (positions depend on wall)
  - Door/window added/changed/removed → host wall dirty (CSG opening changes)
  - Level elevation changed → all elements at that level dirty
  - Stair changed → hosted railings dirty
- Old meshes in `toRemove` are disposed (geometry + materials) to avoid GPU
  leaks. New meshes for `toRebuild` are built and inserted.
- Result: **O(delta) per edit** instead of O(N).

file.content:
### GAP-R5 — Floor slab edge expression

**Description:** The thin horizontal shadow line / projecting plate at the
upper-floor slab level (3 000 mm) is not rendered. This expresses the concrete
or steel edge of the floor slab and is an important articulation in the facade.

Requires: the floor renderer or wall renderer should extrude a thin horizontal
strip at the wall's base elevation (for upper-floor walls) or detect the slab
edge from the floor element.

**Effort:** S — add a thin horizontal strip mesh to `makeWallMesh` when the
wall's reference level elevation > 0.

---

### GAP-R6 — Annex flat roof rendered with wrong colour / slight pitch

**Description:** Annex roof currently uses `slopeDeg: 5` (minimum clamp) and
renders in the same dark reddish-brown as the main gable roof. The sketch
requires a **flat** (0° or near-0°) roof in **light grey/white**.

Two sub-issues:

- `slopeDeg: 5` is the minimum accepted; 0° is rejected. Need either a `flat`
  roof mode or allow `slopeDeg: 0`.
- Roof material is always the roof category colour. Need per-roof colour or a
  flat-roof materialKey.

**Effort:** S — add `roofGeometryMode: "flat"` that bypasses the gable
geometry; add optional `materialKey` on roofs.

---

### GAP-R7 — Interior not visible (staircase, open plan)

**Description:** The sketch shows a floating staircase and interior visible
through the curtain wall. The 3D renderer does not render the interior or stair
as visible-through-glass. The stair element is placed in the model but the
curtain wall glass occludes any interior view.

**Effort:** This is fundamentally a renderer limitation (no interior lighting
pass or glass transparency to interior). Consider as a future aspirational
feature.

---

## 4. Modelling Roadmap

| Priority | Item                                       | Gap ref | Effort    |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------- | --------- |

Here's a self-contained prompt. Copy from the line below the divider down to the end.

---

````markdown
You are working in **`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`** (BIM AI — browser-first BIM authoring kernel; React + Three.js front-end in `packages/web`, TypeScript core in `packages/core`, Python advisor/exporter backend in `app/`).

Implement four backlog items. They are independent, so do them in any order. Each one must meet the project's done rule:
- (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean
- (b) new logic has vitest unit coverage
- (c) `make verify` passes
- (d) committed to a feature branch (do not push or open PRs — leave that to the user)

**Source-of-truth specs (read before starting):**
- `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md` — FL-06 (~line 1456), FL-08 (~line 1533)
- `spec/target-house-seed.md` — GAP-R5 (~line 228), GAP-R7 (~line 261)
- `spec/workpackage-master-tracker.md` — current tracker

**Codebase orientation (verified 2026-05-06):**
- Renderer entry: `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx` (incremental scene manager, `bimPickMapRef`)
- Mesh builders: `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts`
- Materials: `packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts`
- Family catalog: `packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts` (`BUILT_IN_FAMILIES`, `getFamilyById`, `getTypeById`)
- Family geometries: `packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/{doorGeometry,windowGeometry,stairGeometry,railingGeometry}.ts`
- Family editor: `packages/web/src/familyEditor/FamilyEditorWorkbench.tsx`
- Type selector in inspector: `packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx` (~lines 763, 890, 970)
- Element kinds (incl. `wall_type`, `floor_type`, `roof_type`, `family_type`, `WallTypeLayer`): `packages/core/src/index.ts`
- Layer-stack workbench (already exists, edits only — no renderer): `packages/web/src/workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx`

---

## Task 1 — FL-06: Family Library Browser Panel

**Spec:** `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md` lines 1456–1495.
**Branch:** `feat/fl-06-family-library`

**Current state:**
- `BUILT_IN_FAMILIES` in `packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts` already enumerates door / window / stair / railing families with `defaultTypes[]`.
- Inspector `<select>` for `familyTypeId` already shipped (FL-05) at `InspectorContent.tsx:890` (door) and `:970` (window).
- **No** `FamilyLibraryPanel` component exists.

**Build:**
1. `packages/web/src/families/FamilyLibraryPanel.tsx`
   - Reads `BUILT_IN_FAMILIES` plus any `kind: 'family_type'` elements in `elementsById` (custom user types, FL-07).
   - Groups by discipline (`door | window | stair | railing | wall_type | floor_type | roof_type`).
   - Search input filters family + type names (case-insensitive substring).
   - Each type row: thumbnail (128×128), name, "Place" button.
   - Clicking "Place" sets the active draw tool in the tools store with `familyTypeId` pre-loaded; closes the panel.
2. Thumbnail cache module `packages/web/src/families/thumbnailCache.ts`
   - `getThumbnail(typeId): Promise<string /* blob URL */>`.
   - On first call, instantiate one shared offscreen `THREE.WebGLRenderer({ canvas: offscreenCanvas })`, render the appropriate `geometryFns/*` with default-type params under a fixed camera, call `canvas.toBlob()`, `URL.createObjectURL`, cache in module-level `Map<typeId, string>`.
   - Reuse renderer + scene across calls; only swap meshes.
3. Wire in: add a left-rail entry in `packages/web/src/workspace/WorkspaceLeftRail.tsx` ("Families" tab) **and** a command-palette entry in `packages/web/src/cmd/` (search "Browse families") that toggles the panel open.
4. Tests: `packages/web/src/families/FamilyLibraryPanel.test.tsx`
   - Renders all built-in families grouped by discipline.
   - Search "casement" filters down to the casement window family only.
   - Clicking "Place" on a door type calls the tools store with `kind: 'door'` and the right `familyTypeId`.
   - Custom `family_type` elements appear in their discipline group with a "Custom" badge.

**Acceptance:** Panel lists all built-in families grouped by discipline; search filters; clicking Place starts the draw tool with the right `familyTypeId`; thumbnails render within ~200ms (cached after first render).

---

## Task 2 — FL-08: System Family Layer Stack (renderer + plan)

**Spec:** `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md` lines 1533–1574.
**Branch:** `feat/fl-08-wall-layer-render`

**Current state:**
- Data model **done**: `wall_type` element kind in `packages/core/src/index.ts:181`; `WallTypeLayer[]`; walls reference `wallTypeId` at `:220`; `basisLine?: 'center' | 'face_interior' | 'face_exterior'` already on `wall_type`.
- Editor **done**: `MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx` allows authoring layers.
- Renderer **NOT done**: `makeWallMesh` (`meshBuilders.ts:1086`) ignores `wallTypeId` and uses single-thickness `BoxGeometry` plus optional `addCladdingBoards`. No plan-canvas layer lines.

**Build:**
1. `packages/web/src/families/wallTypeCatalog.ts`
   - `BUILT_IN_WALL_TYPES: WallTypeAssembly[]` with the four entries from spec table:
     - `wall.ext-timber` (5 layers totalling 198.5mm)
     - `wall.ext-masonry` (4 layers totalling 290mm)
     - `wall.int-partition` (3 layers totalling 114mm)
     - `wall.int-blockwork` (3 layers totalling 126mm)
   - Each layer has `name`, `thicknessMm`, `materialKey`, `function: 'structure' | 'insulation' | 'finish' | 'membrane' | 'air'`, `exterior?: boolean`.
   - Add to `FamilyLibraryPanel` discipline grouping (Task 1) under "Wall types".
2. `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.layeredWall.ts`
   - `makeLayeredWallMesh(wall, wallType, elevM, paint, elementsById): THREE.Group`
   - Resolve `basisLine`: stack offset starts from centerline (default), interior face, or exterior face.
   - For each layer in order: build `BoxGeometry(thicknessMm/1000, heightM, lengthM)`, position cumulatively along wall normal, apply per-layer `MeshStandardMaterial` from `materials.ts` keyed by `materialKey`.
   - For the exterior-most `function: 'finish'` layer with `materialKey ∈ {timber_cladding, white_cladding}`, call existing `addCladdingBoards`.
   - Air layers (`function: 'air'`) → skip mesh emit but advance offset.
   - All layer meshes get `castShadow = receiveShadow = true`; edges via existing `addEdges` helper.
3. Modify `makeWallMesh` in `meshBuilders.ts:1086`: at the top, look up `elementsById[wall.wallTypeId]` if `wall.wallTypeId` is set; if it resolves to a `wall_type`, dispatch to `makeLayeredWallMesh` and return. Otherwise keep current single-thickness path unchanged.
4. Plan view layer lines:
   - Locate the wall plan-rendering code (likely in `packages/web/src/plan/` or `packages/web/src/workspace/`; grep for `makeWallMesh` consumers and for plan canvas drawing of wall hatch).
   - When `wallTypeId` resolves, draw thin lines parallel to the wall centerline at each cumulative layer boundary (1px stroke, color = layer `materialKey` darkened 30%).
5. Tests:
   - `packages/web/src/families/wallTypeCatalog.test.ts` — 4 entries, total thickness per entry matches spec.
   - `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.layeredWall.test.ts` — `makeLayeredWallMesh` for `wall.ext-timber` returns a group with 4 visible meshes (skip air gap), total bounding-box thickness ≈ 198.5mm, centered on basisLine.

**Acceptance:** A wall with `wallTypeId: 'wall.ext-timber'` renders as a 4-layer extrusion in 3D (cladding + frame+ins + VCL + plasterboard, air gap omitted); total thickness within 0.5mm of 198.5mm; plan view shows layer boundary lines.

---

## Task 3 — GAP-R5: Floor Slab Edge Expression

**Spec:** `spec/target-house-seed.md` lines 228–240.
**Branch:** `feat/gap-r5-slab-edge`

**Build:**
1. Add `floorEdgeStripDisabled?: boolean | null` to the wall element shape in `packages/core/src/index.ts:218` (after `materialKey` on the wall block).
2. In `makeWallMesh` (`meshBuilders.ts:1086`):
   - Resolve the wall's level via `elevationMForLevel(wall.levelId, elementsById)` (already imported in this file, line 78).
   - If `levelElevM > 0` AND `wall.floorEdgeStripDisabled !== true` AND `wall.wallTypeId` is null (single-thickness path only):
     - After the main wall mesh is added to the group, build a thin horizontal strip:
       - Vertical thickness: 50mm (`0.05`)
       - Length: full wall length
       - Projection: `wall.thicknessMm / 1000 + 0.03` (i.e. 30mm beyond exterior face)
       - Y position: at `elevM` (wall base) for 30mm above and 20mm below the slab line
     - Material: new `slabEdge` category in `materials.ts` (`#9a9a92`, roughness 0.6, metalness 0).
     - `castShadow = receiveShadow = true`; add edges via `addEdges`.
3. Test: `packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.slabEdge.test.ts`
   - Wall on level with `elevationMm: 0` → group does **not** contain a slab-edge mesh.
   - Wall on level with `elevationMm: 3000` → group contains exactly one mesh whose Y bounding-box spans roughly `2980 → 3030 mm`.
   - Wall with `floorEdgeStripDisabled: true` → no strip even at elevation > 0.
   - Wall with `wallTypeId` set → no strip (handled inside layer stack instead, deferred).

**Acceptance:** loading the seeded demo house in Viewport, all upper-floor walls (level elevation 3000mm) show a continuous concrete-grey horizontal strip at their base; ground-floor walls don't.

---

## Task 4 — GAP-R7: Interior Visible Through Glass

**Spec:** `spec/target-house-seed.md` lines 261–270.
**Branch:** `feat/gap-r7-interior-through-glass`

**Diagnose first** (this is a renderer fix, not a feature add):

Run the dev server (`pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web run dev` or as the repo's `make dev` target dictates), open the seeded demo house at the SSW viewpoint, and confirm whether the staircase is **(a)** not in the scene, **(b)** in the scene but hidden behind opaque glass, or **(c)** in the scene but z-occluded by transparent glass.

**Build (depends on which case):**

**Case (a) — interior elements skipped from scene:**
- In `Viewport.tsx`, find the geometry useEffect (incremental scene manager). Trace which element kinds get added to `bimPickMapRef`. If `stair`, `room`, or interior walls are filtered when a curtain wall is present, remove that filter.

**Case (b) or (c) — opacity / depth issue (most likely):**
- In `makeCurtainWallMesh` (`meshBuilders.ts:1163`), find the `glassMat` material. Ensure it is `MeshPhysicalMaterial` with:
  - `transparent: true`
  - `transmission: 0.95`
  - `roughness: 0.05`
  - `thickness: 0.005`
  - `depthWrite: false` ← critical; without this, glass writes to z-buffer and occludes anything drawn afterwards regardless of transparency.
  - `side: THREE.DoubleSide`
- Same for the gable-triangle glazing path at `meshBuilders.ts:1282` (uses the same `glassMat`).
- Stair / railing / interior walls keep `depthWrite: true` (default). Three.js's default render order will draw opaque first, then glass — which now does not z-occlude interior.

**Tests:**
1. `packages/web/src/viewport/glassMaterial.test.ts` — assert the curtain wall's glass material has `transparent: true`, `depthWrite: false`, `transmission >= 0.9`.
2. `packages/web/e2e/interior-through-glass.spec.ts` — Playwright:
   - Apply the seeded house, switch to viewpoint `vp-ssw`, take screenshot.
   - Assert pixel-presence of the stair (sample a known pixel region in front of the curtain wall; expect non-glass color).
   - Or use the existing snapshot harness if there is one for rendering baselines.

**Acceptance:** at the SSW viewpoint of the seeded demo, the open-tread staircase is clearly visible through the south curtain wall.

---

## Wrap-up after all four tasks

1. Update `spec/rendering-v2-spec.md`:
   - At top of FL-06 subsection (line ~1456): `**Status:** done (<commit hash>).`
   - At top of FL-08 subsection (line ~1533): same.
2. Update `spec/target-house-seed.md`:
   - In §3, mark GAP-R5 and GAP-R7 with `**Status:** done` notes.
   - Update the §4 "Modelling Roadmap" table — strike R5 / R7 rows or remove them.
3. From repo root: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && make verify` — must be clean.
4. Each task lives on its own branch with a single commit (or a short squashable series). Do **not** push or open PRs.
5. Output a final summary listing each branch + commit hash and any acceptance gaps you couldn't resolve.

If you hit ambiguity (e.g. plan canvas wall-rendering location for FL-08 task 4, or which case GAP-R7 is in), surface a question rather than guessing — the user is available.
````

---

That's the prompt. A few notes for your own awareness:

- I wrote it self-contained because subagents start cold. Trim ruthlessly if you're handing it to a senior agent who can navigate the repo themselves.
- The trickiest task is FL-08 step 4 (plan-canvas layer lines) — I left "grep for `makeWallMesh` consumers" rather than guess at the file, since plan rendering location varies. If you know where wall hatch is drawn, pre-fill that path and the agent will be faster.
- GAP-R7 is genuinely "diagnose first" — the spec called it aspirational because the cause wasn't pinned down. The most likely fix is `depthWrite: false` on glass (very common three.js gotcha), but I left case (a)/(b)/(c) branches in case it's actually scene-graph filtering.

file.content:
**Outcomes:** `ok` on success; `merge_id_collision` / `merge_reference_unresolved` when preflight fails (returns the validated **`commands`** list); `sketch_unavailable` when `available` is not true; `invalid_sketch` for wrong `replayKind` or `schemaVersion`; `invalid_command` for a non-list `commands` or any entry whose `type` is not one of `createLevel` / `createFloor` / `createWall` / `createRoof` / `createStair` / `createRoomOutline` / `insertDoorOnWall` / `insertWindowOnWall` / `createSlabOpening`; `constraint_error` when **`try_commit_bundle`** evaluation hits blocking violations. **`exchange_ifc_ids_identity_pset_gap`** / **`exchange_ifc_ids_qto_gap`** may append a deterministic pointer to **`commandSketch.authoritativeReplay_v0.idsAuthoritativeReplayMap_v0`** (space + roof row counts) when the roundtrip gate runs — not a `quickFixCommand` bundle.

**Still not in v0:** arbitrary IFC importers and unconstrained merge outside this authoritative bundle + preflight. **`summarize_kernel_ifc_semantic_roundtrip`** nests replay under **`commandSketch.authoritativeReplay_v0`**.

## Still deferred

- **IfcRoof** beyond prism replay: **`roofTypeId`** not round-tripped; **`gable_pitched_rectangle`** not distinguished in IFC body; roof-hosted voids remain **`slabRoofHostedVoidReplaySkipped_v0`** only.
- Broader QTO roll-ups (full composite takeoffs), materials, classifications, and layered composites.
- **Draco**/mesh-compression on glTF sibling export (tracked under WP‑X02).

When these land, bump `ifcEncoding` commentary / `kernelNote` in [`export_ifc.py`](../app/bim_ai/export_ifc.py) and extend this table.

## Previous explicit deferrals (history)

- ~~Property sets, quantities, materials, classifications.~~ → **Psets (Reference) shipped; narrow QTO + materials remain.**

## Artifact contract

| Resource                     | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET …/exports/ifc-manifest` | **`ifc_manifest_v0`** JSON: parity fields + `ifcEncoding` + `artifactHasGeometryEntities` + `exportedIfcKindsInArtifact` + `ifcSemanticImportScope_v0` + `kernelExpectedIfcKinds` hints |
| `GET …/exports/model.ifc`    | Downloadable IFC STEP — **`bim_ai_ifc_kernel_v1`** kernel solids when **`ifcopenshell`** + eligible geometry exists; **`bim_ai_ifc_empty_shell_v0`** empty DATA otherwise               |
